subject: english
topic_title: Smell, Taste and Colour, Word Modifiers, and Report Writing
learning_objectives: Use vocabulary related to Smell, Taste and Colour correctly in context.
Apply Word Modifiers; Adjectives and Adverbs correctly in sentences.
Write a clear A Report using the expected features and structure.
Summarize the key ideas in Uganda’s National Resistance Army.

sections.heading: Vocabulary Development – Words Associated with Smell, Taste and Colour
sections.content: Adjectives of Smell: pleasant (fragrant, perfumed, scented, floral, fruity), unpleasant (acrid, fetid, putrid, rancid, pungent). Adjectives of Taste: bitter, piquant, salty, sharp, spicy, stale, garlicky. Adjectives of Colour: amber, lilac, navy, bronze, olive, orchid, vermilion, turquoise.

sections.heading: Structure – Word Modifiers; Adjectives and Adverbs
sections.content: A modifier is a word or phrase added to another word or phrase to give a clearer picture of the latter. Modifiers are usually adjectives and adverbs. Adjectival modifiers describe nouns while adverbial modifiers modify verbs. Examples: Without modifiers: The hunter entered the forest. With modifiers: The hunter entered the forbidden virgin forest.

sections.heading: Writing – A Report
sections.content: A report means an account of a happening. It is similar to a narrative writing. It requires a straight forward account of the event or incident in a simple and uncomplicated language. Format involves: Date, Opening salutation, Title, Narrative paragraphs, Conclusion, Name, Class or Post.

sections.heading: Comprehension – Uganda’s National Resistance Army
sections.content: The passage centers on the account of Uganda’s National Resistance Army (NRA) which was borne as a result of the violence, injustice and the cruelty suffered by the innocent citizens of Uganda during the regimes of Idi Amin and Multi Obote.
